meta.changeRequest: 4 targeted CSS/layout fixes: dropdown nowrap, right-aligned offset, dashed focus outline, fixed header spacer

changes.title: Dropdown items whitespace-nowrap
changes.description: Add whitespace-nowrap to dropdown submenu item links so text does not wrap to multiple lines.

files.detail: Add `whitespace-nowrap` to the ThemeLink className string. Current className is `'py-2 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase no-underline! hover:text-accent transition-colors block focus-visible:text-accent focus-visible:outline-solid focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2'`. Add `whitespace-nowrap` to it.

Add `whitespace-nowrap` to the Link className string. Current className is `'py-2 font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.16] text-white uppercase no-underline! hover:text-accent transition-colors block focus-visible:text-accent focus-visible:outline-solid focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2'`. Add `whitespace-nowrap` to it.

changes.title: Right-aligned dropdown offset with -mr-6
changes.description: When submenuAlignment is 'right', the dropdown needs -mr-6 to mirror the left-aligned behavior that uses -ml-6. The dropdown has p-6 padding, so the negative margin offsets the padding to align the content edge with the parent.

files.detail: In the submenu div class string, the conditional currently outputs `md:right-0` for right alignment. Change this to also include `md:-mr-6` when right-aligned. The current ternary is: `$submenuAlignment === 'right' ? 'md:right-0' : 'md:left-0'`. Change to: `$submenuAlignment === 'right' ? 'md:right-0 md:-mr-6' : 'md:left-0'`.

In the submenu div's cn() call, add `md:-mr-6` to the right-aligned conditional. Currently there are two conditions: `'md:left-0': attributes.submenuAlignment === 'left'` and `'md:right-0': attributes.submenuAlignment === 'right'`. Change the right condition to `'md:right-0 md:-mr-6': attributes.submenuAlignment === 'right'`.

changes.title: Change focus-visible outline from solid to dashed
changes.description: Replace all instances of `focus-visible:outline-solid` with `focus-visible:outline-dashed` across all Header sub-block files. This affects interactive elements: links, buttons, and submenu items.

files.detail: Two occurrences: the ThemeLink className (line 29) and the button className (line 39).
Three occurrences: the Link className (line 108), the button cn() string (line 117), and the inner Link inside button does not have outline (confirmed). Check all instances.
One occurrence in the ThemeLink className (line 21).
One occurrence in the Link className (line 42).

changes.title: Fixed header spacer to prevent content shift
changes.description: When the header becomes position:fixed on scroll, it leaves the document flow and the page content jumps up. A spacer element is needed that occupies the header's height when it is fixed, preventing layout shift. The JS already tracks `--header-main-height` via a ResizeObserver/resize listener (HeaderMain.js line 166-173). The spacer should be a sibling div that becomes visible (with height = var(--header-main-height)) when the header has the `.scrolling` class.
changes.approach: Add a spacer div as a sibling AFTER the header-main div in HeaderMain.php. The spacer uses CSS to respond to the header's scrolling state. When the `.header-main` element has the `.scrolling` class, the spacer should take up the header height. Use the existing `--header-main-height` CSS variable that the JS already sets.

files.detail:
Add a spacer div immediately after the closing `` of the `.header-main` div (before the outer `` that closes the block wrapper). The spacer should be: `
`. This uses the sibling combinator (~) so when header-main has .scrolling, the spacer gets the header height. The spacer needs to be inside the block wrapper div but after the header-main div. The existing `--header-main-height` variable is already set by HeaderMain.js on `document.documentElement`, so reference it. Alternative simpler approach: set the spacer height directly in HeaderMain.js when scrolling class is toggled.

In the JS, after the header-main element gets the `.scrolling` class added (line 110), also find the spacer element (`.header-main-spacer` sibling) and set its height. When `.scrolling` is removed (line 150-153), reset the spacer height to 0. Specifically: 1) At initialization (around line 249), query the spacer: `const spacer = header.parentElement?.querySelector('.header-main-spacer');` 2) In updateScrollStatus, when adding 'scrolling' class: `if (spacer) spacer.style.height = header.offsetHeight + 'px';` 3) When removing 'scrolling' class: `if (spacer) spacer.style.height = '0';` This is simpler and more reliable than CSS-only with sibling selectors.

Add a spacer div after the header-main div inside the block wrapper. This is for editor preview consistency. The spacer div: `<div className="header-main-spacer h-0" />`. The editor's scroll behavior also manages the scrolling class, so the spacer should be present in the editor too.

decisions.decision: Use whitespace-nowrap on submenu item links
decisions.evidence: User explicitly requested dropdown items no-wrap

decisions.decision: Add md:-mr-6 to right-aligned dropdowns to mirror the existing md:-ml-6 on left-aligned dropdowns
decisions.evidence: User specified that dropdown has p-6 padding so right-aligned needs -mr-6 to offset, mirroring left-aligned -ml-6

decisions.decision: Change focus-visible outline style from solid to dashed across all header interactive elements
decisions.evidence: User explicitly requested focus-visible:outline-dashed instead of focus-visible:outline-solid

decisions.decision: Use a JS-driven spacer element to prevent content shift when header becomes fixed
decisions.evidence: User described the content shift problem and requested a spacer/placeholder. JS approach chosen because the existing HeaderMain.js already tracks header height and scroll state, making it the natural place to manage the spacer.

testPage.note: Existing test page - no changes needed to test page content. All changes are CSS/JS behavioral and can be verified on the existing page.
